title: alyssum taygeteum
intro: Alyssum taygeteum, also known as White Horehound or Greek Horehound, is a perennial plant species in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Alyssum taygeteum include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. Alyssum taygeteum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
